title: 10 best herbal creams for inflammatory bowel disease

remedy_properties: Curcumin: A polyphenol compound found in turmeric, curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, thereby alleviating IBD symptoms.

Corticosteroids-like activity: Turmeric creams contain compounds that have a similar structure to corticosteroids, which are commonly used to treat IBD. These compounds can reduce inflammation and swelling in the gut by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and proteins.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties: The antioxidants present in turmeric creams, such as vitamin C and E, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, reducing damage to gut tissue and improving IBD symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin, found in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, which are key factors in the development and progression of inflammatory bowel disease. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, curcuma longa creams may help alleviate symptoms of IBD, such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and weight loss, providing relief and improving quality of life for those affected.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids (especially Curcumin): These polyphenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Tumerones and Sesquiterpenoids (e.g., Curcumenol and Curcumenone): These terpenes have shown anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the gut, which is beneficial for patients with inflammatory bowel disease.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): This curcuminoid has been found to have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, as well as inhibit the growth of certain pathogens that may contribute to inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Curcuma longa are widely used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound.
Roots: Curcuma longa roots are also used for their anti-inflammatory properties, as they contain a significant amount of curcuminoids.
Stems: The stems of Curcuma longa, although less commonly used, may be employed for their residual curcumin content, although to a lesser extent compared to rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 large roots of curcuma longa and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Dry the roots in a warm place for 7 days to reduce moisture content to 10 percent.
Grind 50 grams of dried curcuma longa root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of coconut oil with 50 grams of curcuma longa powder and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens.

remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm's mucilage property creates a protective barrier on the gut lining, soothing inflammation and reducing irritation in individuals with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) by providing a physical barrier against irritants.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herbal slippery elm cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with IBD such as pain, bloating, and cramping.

Prebiotic properties: Slippery elm's prebiotic fibers help feed the good bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and supporting the body's natural defense against inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because it contains mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the gastrointestinal tract. This barrier helps to soothe and calm inflammation, reducing symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and rectal bleeding. By promoting the healing of the intestinal lining, Ulmus rubra creams may also help to improve nutrient absorption and reduce the frequency of bowel movements, providing relief for individuals suffering from inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins in Ulmus rubra may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to inflammatory bowel disease.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, found in Ulmus rubra may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gut from inflammation and damage.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as gallic acid, found in Ulmus rubra may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and infection in the gut.
remedy_parts: Barks: It's used to make creams due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Leaves: They are used in creams for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Stems: Stems of Ulmus rubra are used in creams due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried ulmus rubra bark by cutting it from mature trees in autumn.
Combine 1 tablespoon of dried bark with 2 cups of carrier oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, to infuse the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and chromone, which help reduce inflammation in the gut by inhibiting prostaglandins and leukotrienes, leading to relief from IBD symptoms.

Moisturizing and soothing properties: The gel of the aloe plant has moisturizing and soothing properties that help to heal damaged mucous membranes in the gut, reducing symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping associated with IBD.

Antioxidant activity: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and progression of IBD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel from the aloe plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract. Additionally, aloe vera creams can help to reduce symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), such as abdominal pain and diarrhea, by coating and protecting the intestinal lining, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut environment.
remedy_constituents: Acemannan: This polysaccharide constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of IBD.

Aloin: This anthraquinone glycoside has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help protect the gut lining, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections that can exacerbate IBD.

Saponins: These triterpenoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help regulate the immune system's response and reduce inflammation in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of IBD.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of Aloe vera gel, which contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with inflammatory bowel disease.

Gel from Leaves: The clear gel extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera contains mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract.

Juice from Leaves: The juice extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help promote healing and reduce inflammation in the digestive system, making it beneficial for inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of aloe vera gel from 3 mature aloe vera plants.
Weigh out 10 grams of beeswax and melt in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of shea butter with the melted beeswax.
Combine 50 grams of aloe vera gel and 20 grams of distilled water with the oil mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of IBD such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and bloody stools.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in herbal licorice creams have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the gut lining from damage, reducing inflammation and improving overall gut health.

Isoflavones: Isoflavones, such as formononetin and calycosin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in individuals with IBD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The triterpenoid saponin compounds present in licorice root have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the inflammation and damage seen in conditions like Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis. By reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the gut lining, licorice creams may help alleviate symptoms and improve quality of life for individuals with inflammatory bowel disease. This has led to its use as a complementary therapy.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin helps with inflammatory bowel disease by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune system, which in turn alleviates symptoms of conditions like Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis.

Licoricidin (Licorice Root Flavonoid): Licoricidin helps with inflammatory bowel disease by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which play a significant role in the inflammation and damage associated with inflammatory bowel diseases.

Licopyrones (Glycyrrhetinic Acid): Licopyrones help with inflammatory bowel disease by reducing inflammation and modulating the gut microbiome, which in turn alleviates symptoms of conditions like irritable bowel syndrome and ulcerative colitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizic acid, which is used to make creams for inflammatory bowel disease due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots also contain flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gut.
Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain saponins, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots at the end of summer when the plant is in full maturity stage.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place for 7 to 10 days to remove moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 100 grams of petroleum jelly and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 10 to 15 minutes until the beeswax melts and the mixture becomes smooth.

remedy_properties: Mucilage content: The mucilages present in herbal marshmallow creams create a soothing, protective barrier on the gut lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with IBD.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow herbs contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of IBD.

Gut-soothing saponins: Saponins found in marshmallow herbs have been shown to have a calming effect on the gut lining, reducing inflammation and improving digestion, which can be beneficial for individuals with IBD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis, also known as marshmallow, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root of the Althaea plant contains mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates that form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes. This helps to reduce inflammation, calm irritated tissues, and promote healing in the digestive tract. By protecting and soothing the lining of the gut, Althaea officinalis creams can help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease, such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and rectal bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help soothe and protect the gut lining, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in individuals with inflammatory bowel disease.

Iridoids (e.g., altheaflavone and altheanone): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Mucilages (e.g., pectin and rhamnogalacturonan): These soluble fibers help form a protective barrier on the gut lining, reducing inflammation, and preventing bacterial translocation, which can contribute to the development of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and calm the digestive tract.
Leaves: They contain similar mucilage properties as the roots and can contribute to the soothing and anti-inflammatory effects of the creams.
Barks: Althaea officinalis barks also contain mucilage, which is extracted and used to create creams that can help alleviate digestive issues and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried root of althaea officinalis from a reliable source and clean the area thoroughly.
Weigh out 10g of beeswax and 10g of cocoa butter for the cream base in a double boiler.
Combine 10g of sweet almond oil and 10g of jojoba oil with the beeswax and cocoa butter mixture.
Gradually add 10g of distilled water to the mixture and stir until the cream is fully incorporated.
Pour the herbal cream into glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of IBD such as abdominal pain and diarrhea.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in devil's claw cream may help protect the gut lining from oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic inflammation, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in the digestive system.

Gut-soothing mucilage: Devil's claw cream contains a thick, protective mucilage that can help soothe and coat the gastrointestinal tract, providing relief from symptoms of IBD such as cramping, bloating, and abdominal pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Harpagophytum procumbens, also known as devil's claw, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, including harpagoside and iridoid glycosides, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with IBD, such as abdominal pain and diarrhea. By modulating the immune system and increasing blood flow to the digestive tract, Harpagophytum procumbens creams can help to promote healing and reduce the severity of IBD symptoms, providing relief for individuals suffering from this condition.
remedy_constituents: Harpagoside: This iridoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Harpagophytum procumbens phenolics: These polyphenolic compounds exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, protecting the gut lining from damage and reducing inflammation associated with inflammatory bowel disease.

Harpagoside-related terpenes: The terpene content in Harpagophytum procumbens, particularly the sesquiterpenes, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, helping to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high content of harpagoside, a bioactive compound responsible for its anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, are also used due to their rich content of harpagoside, which is believed to help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Seeds: The seeds may be used as a complementary part, potentially contributing to the overall efficacy of the cream, although their role in inflammatory bowel disease management is less well-documented.
remedy_recipe: Harvest harpagophytum procumbens roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full maturity stage.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols, shogaols, and paradols that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a common symptom of IBD.

Digestive enzyme stimulation: The active ingredients in ginger creams can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, such as pepsin and gastric amylase, which helps improve digestion and absorption of nutrients in people with IBD.
Antioxidant activity: Ginger creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and damage to the gut lining, which is exacerbated by chronic inflammation in IBD patients.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with IBD. Ginger creams may also help to regulate gut motility and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development and progression of IBD. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties can help to protect the gut lining from damage and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gastrointestinal tract associated with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).
Shogaols: Shogaols have been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gut lining, which is often damaged in IBD patients.

Volatiles (6-Gingerol and 6-Shogaol): These volatiles exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that help reduce inflammation in the gut, alleviate symptoms of IBD, and promote the healing of the gut lining.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing inflammation associated with inflammatory bowel disease.

Roots: Roots are used due to their rich content of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which help in alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease by reducing inflammation and promoting digestion.

Barks: Although less commonly used, barks are sometimes used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in soothing and healing the gastrointestinal tract affected by inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 1 cup of beeswax pellets and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine 2 tablespoons of distilled water and 4 tablespoons of glycerin with the beeswax mixture.
Add 2 teaspoons of zingiber officinale essential oil and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to mixture.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes and maintain temperature at 120 to 140 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the mixture into 8 oz containers and let them cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, methyl salicylate, and camphor, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of IBD such as abdominal pain and diarrhea.

Relaxation of smooth muscle: The menthol content in peppermint creams has a natural relaxing effect on smooth muscle tissue in the gut, which can help reduce spasms and cramps associated with IBD, allowing for more comfortable bowel movements.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Peppermint creams contain antioxidants like vitamin E and beta-carotene, as well as antimicrobial compounds that can help combat harmful bacteria and oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to inflammation and exacerbate IBD symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of IBD. The menthol in peppermint creams helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can become spasmodic and painful in people with IBD. Additionally, peppermint's antioxidant properties help to protect the gut lining and promote healing, making it a popular natural remedy for IBD sufferers to manage their symptoms and improve their quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut associated with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).
Linalool: This terpene has shown anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of IBD by reducing inflammation, bacterial overgrowth, and gut permeability.

Menthol: This terpene has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help provide relief from abdominal pain and discomfort associated with IBD, as well as reduce inflammation and promote gut healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help relieve digestive issues.
Stems: Utilized for their similar properties to leaves, including menthol and menthone content, which aid in reducing inflammation and soothing digestive discomfort.
Roots: Employed for their rich content of sesquiterpenes and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh leaves and flowers of mentha x piperita in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Steep the harvested plant material in 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 40 degrees celsius for four hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean container.
Blend the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Remove the cream from the heat and allow it to cool and thicken before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin, which helps reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of IBD such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in chamomile cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage in the digestive tract, which is common in IBD patients.

Gastroprotective properties: Chamomile cream's gastroprotective properties help soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the gut, reducing the risk of irritation, bleeding, and perforation, which can occur in severe cases of IBD.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gastrointestinal tract. These compounds have also been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby reducing the severity of symptoms associated with inflammatory bowel disease, including abdominal pain and diarrhea. Additionally, chamomile creams may also help soothe and calm the digestive tract, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Luteolin: A flavonoid that inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators, which are often elevated in inflammatory bowel disease, thus reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

α-Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the gut, providing relief from symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most used part of Matricaria chamomilla to make creams for inflammatory bowel disease because of their high content of apigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Although not as commonly used as flowers, the leaves are also used to make creams for inflammatory bowel disease due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla are occasionally used in creams for inflammatory bowel disease, possibly due to their apigenin content and potential anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers on a sunny day in mid-summer.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place at 35°c for 24 hours.
Steep 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 100g of coconut oil at 60°c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix 10g of beeswax with the strained oil and heat the mixture at 60°c until it melts.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to conditions like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and Crohn's disease.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the gut lining, which can alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping.

Digestive enzyme inhibitors: Fennel has been shown to inhibit certain digestive enzymes that contribute to inflammation and damage in the gut, such as proteases and lipases, helping to reduce symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to reduce inflammation and modulate the gut microbiome. This can help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease, such as abdominal pain and diarrhea, by promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing oxidative stress. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to ease bloating and discomfort, providing relief for individuals with IBD.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole, a major terpene compound in fennel, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut associated with inflammatory bowel disease.

Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a phenolic compound found in fennel, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease by reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial overgrowth in the gut.

Limonene: Limonene, a terpene compound in fennel, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gut from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
Seeds: They are also used due to their carminative properties, which can help reduce gas and alleviate digestive discomfort associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Clean and dry the harvested material in a single layer at room temperature for 24 hours.
Steep 150 grams of the dried material in 1 liter of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Combine 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of preservative with the infused oil and mix thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated bowels. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and triterpenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, alleviating symptoms of conditions like Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis. Additionally, dandelion's natural demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the intestinal lining, further reducing irritation and discomfort associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of IBD such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping.
Antioxidant activity: Dandelion's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage the gut lining and exacerbate IBD symptoms, promoting healing and reducing oxidative stress.

Prebiotic fiber content: Dandelion contains prebiotic fibers like inulin, which help feed beneficial gut bacteria, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and alleviating symptoms of IBD such as bloating, gas, and abdominal discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, making them effective in soothing symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the dandelion plant contains compounds like taraxasterol and taraxanthin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful for managing inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality fresh dandelion roots taraxacum officinale.
Grind 200 grams of dried root into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of powdered root with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at low temperature to infuse the herbs into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and package it in an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains compounds like pterostilbene, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and weight loss. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help protect the gut lining from damage, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and improving overall digestive function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Maidenhair Tree creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of IBD.
Antioxidant properties: The creams' antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate IBD symptoms by protecting the gut lining from damage and reducing oxidative stress.

Gut-soothing mucilages: Maidenhair Tree creams contain mucilage-rich compounds that provide a soothing, protective coating to the inflamed gut lining, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with IBD.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair Tree contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the gut, making them effective for soothing symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Maidenhair Tree have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can aid in managing inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves at dawn or early morning when their potency is highest.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator set to 60°c for 8 hours to preserve bioactive compounds.
Combine 20% dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 80% sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 75°c for 30 minutes, then stir occasionally until the ingredients are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool before storing it in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream contains eucalyptol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease. Additionally, the cream's soothing and emollient properties help to calm irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. By addressing both inflammatory and irritant responses, Tasmanian blue gum creams offer a natural and effective approach to managing symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, a primary constituent of Tasmanian Blue Gum oil, has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of IBD such as abdominal pain and diarrhea.
Cineole: Cineole, another main component of the oil, exhibits strong antioxidant properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation in IBD.

Gum resin: The gum resin of Tasmanian Blue Gum contains a high concentration of polyphenols, which have been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in cases of inflammatory bowel disease.
Barks: The barks have been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal issues, including irritable bowel syndrome, due to their ability to soothe and calm the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh or dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the ground eucalyptus globulus with 200 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at 70°c to 80°c for 1 hour.
Add 10 drops of lavender essential oil and 5 drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir well for 30 minutes.
Heat the mixture at 60°c to 70°c for an additional 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture has reached a smooth consistency.
Pour the warm mixture into sterile glass jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4°c to 8°c for up to 6 months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, saponins, and carotenoids. These bioactive molecules work synergistically to reduce inflammation in the gut lining, soothing irritated tissues and alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloody stools. Additionally, pot marigold's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and promote healing, further enhancing its beneficial effects on inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the gut lining and alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) like diarrhea, abdominal pain, and cramping.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams have been shown to neutralize free radicals that can damage gut tissues and contribute to IBD, helping to promote healing and protect against further inflammation.

Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold creams contain triterpenoids, which have been found to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and reducing scar formation - all of which can be beneficial for individuals with IBD who may experience chronic wounds or mucosal damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with inflammatory bowel disease.

Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain a high concentration of carotenoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, making them beneficial for treating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry calendula flowers according to traditional methods within 24 hours of blooming at dawn.
Grind dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to achieve 20 grams per recipe.
Combine the ground calendula powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low heat for 30 minutes, stirring frequently, until the beeswax melts fully and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool to room temperature, then pour it into sterilized jars for storage at refrigerator temperature within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its rich isoflavone content. Isoflavones have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract, making red clover a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of conditions such as Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis. The cream can be applied topically to soothe irritated skin and mucous membranes, while also providing systemic benefits by being absorbed into the bloodstream, reducing inflammation from within. This natural approach has been effective in managing flare-ups and improving overall quality of life for individuals with inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly formononetin and biochanin A, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogenic compounds in red clover cream, such as isoflavones, have estrogen-like effects that can help to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms of IBD by binding to estrogen receptors in the gut and modulating the immune response.

Antioxidants: Red clover cream contains a high amount of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of IBD such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the red clover plant contain isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in inflammatory bowel disease.
Seeds: Red clover seeds contain a compound called triterpenoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh white clover flowers, then air-dry them for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried flowers with 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 6 hours at low heat to infuse the herbs.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth, discarding solids, then add 1g of beeswax.
Pour the mixture into containers, let cool and harden, and store at room temperature for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because they contain fatty acids that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated bowels and calm inflammation, providing relief from symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and constipation. Additionally, saw palmetto has been shown to improve digestion and reduce inflammation in the gut lining, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and improving overall bowel function.
remedy_properties: Serenoide content: Saw palmetto contains serenoides, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that may help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of IBD.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in saw palmetto creams can help protect the gastrointestinal lining from oxidative damage, reducing inflammation and mucosal damage associated with IBD.
5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto has been shown to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme involved in the production of pro-inflammatory molecules that contribute to IBD pathophysiology.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the saw palmetto plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflamed bowels and reduce symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant are rich in fatty acids and phytosterols, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, making them an effective ingredient for medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried serenoa repens leaves and flowers from reputable sources for quality and purity.
Mix the dried herbs with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm water bath at 50°c to 60°c for 30 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil to the infused oil, heating gently until melted and fully incorporated.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for inflammatory bowel disease are topical treatments derived from plants that help alleviate symptoms such as inflammation, pain, and discomfort associated with the condition. They work by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The best herbal creams for inflammatory bowel disease include Curcuma longa, Aloe barbadensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Ulmus rubra, Symphytum officinale, Aloe vera, Calendula officinalis, Althaea officinalis, and Plantago ovata. If left untreated, inflammatory bowel disease can severely impact daily life, causing chronic diarrhea, abdominal pain, weight loss, and fatigue, leading to social isolation, decreased productivity, and a reduced quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to inflammatory bowel disease that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, acne, and psoriasis. These conditions often involve inflammation and skin lesions, which can be relieved by topical applications of herbal creams containing anti-inflammatory ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil. These herbs have been traditionally used to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching associated with these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with inflammatory bowel disease is a blend of aloe vera, calendula, and chamomile. Aloe vera soothes and calms the digestive tract, while calendula reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Chamomile, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to relax the gut and alleviate symptoms of IBD. These creams can be applied topically to the abdomen or ingested as a supplement, providing relief from symptoms such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and constipation associated with inflammatory bowel disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound. Curcumin has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, which are key factors in the development of IBD. By inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines, curcuma longa creams may help to reduce symptoms of IBD, such as abdominal pain, diarrhea, and weight loss. Additionally, curcumin's antioxidant properties help to protect the gut lining and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): This constituent has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the development and progression of inflammatory bowel disease.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties, which help protect the gut lining from damage and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most used part due to their high concentration of curcumin, a polyphenol with potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark's rich curcumin content and other compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
Roots: The root's high curcumin content and other bioactive compounds provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits, making it an effective component in creams for inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200g of curcuma longa rhizomes by peeling and chopping them into small pieces for 20 minutes.
Infuse 500ml of water with the chopped rhizomes in a saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused water through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids immediately.
Combine 100g of beeswax and 200g of coconut oil in a saucepan and melt over low heat for 15 minutes.
Mix the infused curcuma longa water with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and whip until thickened.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract. Additionally, Aloe barbadensis has been found to have prebiotic properties, helping to balance the gut microbiome and alleviate symptoms of IBD, such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and weight loss.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of the gastrointestinal tract, thus alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of inflammatory bowel disease symptoms such as inflammation and pain.
Glycoproteins: These help to reduce inflammation and promote the healing of the gastrointestinal tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe barbadensis due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin.

Leaves (gel): The gel extracted from the leaves is particularly useful in creams for inflammatory bowel disease because it contains soothing compounds like vitamins A, C, and E, as well as amino acids and other nutrients.

Leaves (aloe vera extract): Aloe vera extract, often derived from the leaves, is used in creams due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of aloe barbadensis gel and mix it with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt 20g of coconut oil and 10g of shea butter in the double boiler.
Combine the aloe barbadensis mixture with the melted coconut oil and shea butter mixture.
Add 10g of vitamin e oil and stir the mixture thoroughly for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because they contain licorice root extract, which has potent anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties. The extract reduces inflammation and mucosal damage in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the disease's progression. This natural remedy has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various gastrointestinal disorders, including inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
Glycyrrhizin: This saponin compound exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, which help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in the gut.

Isoflavans: These compounds, including glabrene and isoglabrene, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to protect the gut mucosa and reduce inflammation associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that helps reduce inflammation in the gut.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain glycosides and flavonoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which aid in soothing bowel inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their richness in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200g of dried roots with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin to obtain the active compound.
Filter the extract to remove the residue and achieve a clear liquid consistency within 30 minutes.
Mix the filtered extract with 100g of cream base and 20g of emulsifying wax in a 100ml beaker.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70-80°c for 10-15 minutes to dissolve the wax and emulsify.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30-60 minutes before filling into containers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gastrointestinal tract. This can lead to improved symptoms and quality of life for individuals with inflammatory bowel disease, including reduced pain, cramping, and diarrhea. Additionally, ginger has been found to have antimicrobial properties, helping to manage the gut microbiome and alleviate symptoms of IBD.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
Shogaols: Shogaols have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thus reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gastrointestinal tract.

Curcuminoids: These bioactive compounds possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginger contain gingerol and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with IBD.
Buds: The buds of ginger contain bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help manage IBD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Grate 50g of the washed zingiber officinale using a fine grater and set aside for 2 minutes.
Combine the grated zingiber officinale with 50g of beeswax pellets in a double boiler and heat for 10 minutes.
Add 100ml of coconut oil and 50ml of distilled water to the double boiler and heat for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory properties, derived from the Ulmus rubra tree. The cream's active compounds, such as salicin and tannins, help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the digestive tract. By calming inflammation and promoting healing, Ulmus rubra creams may alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, and bloody stools. This natural remedy supports the body's natural healing processes, providing relief and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
Tannins: Tannins can help protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation, improve gut barrier function, and alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Barks: Ulmus rubra barks are used in traditional medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help soothe and calm inflamed tissues.
Leaves: Ulmus rubra leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce inflammation and promote healing in creams for inflammatory bowel disease.
Buds: Ulmus rubra buds are used in traditional medicine due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help soothe and calm inflamed tissues, and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of dried ulmus rubra bark and 15g of beeswax at dawn when dew is still present.
Mix 100g of jojoba oil with 25g of shea butter in a double boiler at 60c temperature for 30 minutes.
Combine the mixture from step 2 with 15g of vitamin e oil and 10g of essential oil in a stainless steel bowl.
Stir the mixture from step 3 with a wooden spoon for 5 minutes and add 25g of the dried ulmus rubra bark.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify for 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compound, allantoin, has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair in the gut. This can lead to improved symptoms and reduced pain in individuals with inflammatory bowel disease. Additionally, the cream's demulcent properties help protect and soothe the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, promoting a healthy gut environment and facilitating the healing process. This can result in improved digestion and reduced inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing in the gastrointestinal tract, potentially alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
Rutin: Contributes by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, which may help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.

Symphytine (Caffeic acid derivative): Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gastrointestinal tract, potentially alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of allantoin, which helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
Stems: The stems are used for their mucilaginous properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried symphytum officinale leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Combine the dried herb with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every other day.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 50g of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Aloe vera also has antioxidant properties, which help protect the gut from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, it promotes the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, supporting a healthy gut microbiome and alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease, such as abdominal pain and diarrhea.
remedy_constituents: Acemannan: This polysaccharide helps to reduce inflammation in the gut by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria and enhancing the immune system's response to inflammatory stimuli.
Glucomannan: This soluble fiber helps to soothe and protect the gut lining, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, such as diarrhea and abdominal pain.

Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the high concentration of gel-like substance (aloe vera gel) which has anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and heal gut inflammation.
Stems: The inner part of the stem contains a high amount of acemannan, which stimulates the immune system to fight off inflammation in the gut.
(Optional) Barks: Some research suggests that the bark of the Aloe vera plant has anti-inflammatory properties, though it's less commonly used in creams compared to the leaves and stems.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of aloe vera gel from 3-4 aloe vera leaves and store in the refrigerator for 30 minutes.
Combine 100g of aloe vera gel with 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler or microwave for 2-3 minutes at 30-second intervals.
Add 5g of vitamin e oil and 5g of shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container and let it cool and set for 1-2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated tissues in the digestive tract. The plant's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, Calendula officinalis creams may help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease, including abdominal pain, diarrhea, and rectal bleeding, providing relief and promoting overall digestive health. Its natural anti-inflammatory action makes it a valuable adjunct therapy.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: Help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut by modulating the immune response and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Phenolic acids (including caffeic acid, ferulic acid, and rosmarinic acid): Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe the gastrointestinal tract and reduce the severity of inflammatory bowel disease symptoms.
N-Acetyltyrosol (NAT): Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gut, thus alleviating symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Calendula officinalis due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory compounds and antioxidants.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Stems: The stems are used to provide a structural component to the cream, allowing the anti-inflammatory compounds from the flowers and leaves to be effectively absorbed.
remedy_recipe: Steep 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
Combine the infused calendula liquid with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens and becomes a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because they provide soothing and anti-inflammatory properties to the digestive tract. The mucilages present in the marshmallow root create a protective barrier, reducing inflammation and irritation. This helps to calm the gut lining and prevent further damage, allowing the body to heal naturally. Additionally, Althaea officinalis creams have been traditionally used to help manage symptoms such as diarrhea, abdominal pain, and ulceration associated with inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: They help with inflammatory bowel disease by forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract.
Polysaccharides: They help with inflammatory bowel disease by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which can aid in reducing inflammation and improving gut health.
Flavonoids: They help with inflammatory bowel disease by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties to the digestive tract.
Leaves: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive system.
Stems: Employed for their mucilaginous properties, which can help calm and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried althaea officinalis root and clean it thoroughly for 10 minutes.
Infuse 50g of the cleaned root in 500ml of water at 100°c for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth and collect the liquid for 5 minutes.
Mix 5% beeswax and 10% coconut oil with the strained liquid for 5 minutes.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and reaches a smooth cream consistency in 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago ovata creams helps with inflammatory bowel disease because they contain mucilage, a protective and soothing compound that forms a physical barrier on the intestinal lining. This barrier helps to reduce inflammation, prevent bacterial invasion, and promote healing in the gut. The anti-inflammatory properties of mucilage also help to calm and soothe the digestive system, reducing symptoms such as abdominal pain and diarrhea associated with inflammatory bowel disease, making it a potential natural remedy for this condition.
remedy_constituents: Mucilage: Helps with inflammatory bowel disease by forming a protective barrier on the gut lining, reducing inflammation, and preventing the adhesion of pathogens and toxins.

Phenolic acids: Contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Plantago ovata, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the gut, which can exacerbate inflammatory bowel disease.

Triterpenoids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response in the gut, which can help alleviate symptoms of inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and protect the digestive tract.
Leaves: They are used because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the digestive system.
Roots: They are used due to their ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, which can contribute to inflammatory bowel disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part plantago ovata leaves and 1 part plantago ovata seeds and dry them for 2 weeks in a warm dark place.
Crush the dried leaves and seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of a carrier oil such as coconut oil and heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove any solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 2 hours before applying it to the skin.
